tax: Domain-ranked origin systems and editorial guides begin beneath this root. Reference and Catalogue tags are editorial statuses, not biological ranks.
ap: The root has no organismal body. Its display value is the interface structure: index tree, rank badges, tabs, filters, warning marks, and entry panels.
eco: Use the root to move among origin systems, transformations, deities, and guide pages without implying that all entries share one biological ancestor.
beh: The root has no behavior. Its editorial role is to keep taxonomy, transformation pathways, divine entities, and reference material separated.
traitsText: Root-level traits are catalogue traits: stable IDs, rank badges, tags, generated entry numbers, tree filters, tabs, and validation marks.
abilities: Root-level abilities are interface abilities only: navigation, filtering, display, and validation. Creature abilities belong on specific entries.
bg: The root exists so the Throat record can be revised branch by branch without losing the distinction between taxa, transformations, deities, and guides.
g: The catalogue root is a navigation container for the field journal rather than a biological ancestor or taxological rank.
